scenario_description: Three models walk together wearing distinct scarves; in a subsequent shot, the middle model wears a coat but holds the striped scarf, while the polka dot scarf has moved to the right model. The final question requires tracking the polka dot scarf's location across these changes.

turns.text: Here are the three models walking down the street. They're all wearing different patterned scarves.
turns.image_description: A wide shot of three female models walking side-by-side on a city sidewalk during daylight. The model on the far left wears a grey trench coat and a red-and-white plaid scarf. The model in the center wears a beige sweater and a bright blue scarf with white horizontal stripes. The model on the far right wears a black leather jacket and a yellow scarf with large black polka dots. All three are smiling and looking forward.

turns.text: Nice lineup! I like how the patterns contrast against their coats. That blue striped one really pops in the middle.
Got a closer look at the middle one now. She changed her top but kept holding onto that scarf.
turns.image_description: A medium close-up of the model who was previously in the center. She is now wearing a solid black wool coat instead of the beige sweater. In her hands, she is clutching the bright blue scarf with white horizontal stripes. The background is blurred, showing only hints of the street. The other two models are not visible in this frame.

turns.text: That black coat looks sharp on her. It's good she didn't lose the blue striped scarf!
Check this out. Someone passed the yellow one over.
turns.image_description: A medium shot focusing on the model who was originally on the far right. She is still wearing the black leather jacket. She is now holding the yellow scarf with large black polka dots in front of her chest. The model on the left (plaid scarf) is partially visible in the background on the left edge, but the center model is out of frame. The lighting suggests it is slightly later in the day than the first photo.

turns.text: Oh, so the yellow polka dot one made its way over there? Looks like they are swapping accessories around.

Based on what we've seen, where is the polka dot scarf located right now?
A) On the model who started on the left
B) On the model who started in the middle
C) On the model who started on the right
D) It is no longer visible

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. Turn 1 establishes the initial positions: Left=Plaid, Middle=Striped, Right=Polka Dot. 2. Turn 3 confirms the Middle model still has the Striped scarf. 3. Turn 5 shows the Right model (identifiable by the black leather jacket from Turn 1) now holding the Polka Dot scarf. 4. Therefore, the polka dot scarf is currently with the model who started on the right.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the specific identity of the 'Right' model across turns despite changes in framing and the absence of the other models in later shots. A model relying only on the last image might guess incorrectly if it doesn't link the 'black leather jacket' back to the original 'Right' position, or confuse the scarves if it misremembers the initial assignment.

_meta.scenario: Turn one features three models walking together, each wearing a distinct patterned scarf (stripes, polka dots, plaid). Turn two focuses on a close-up of the model who was originally in the middle, now wearing a solid black coat but still holding the striped scarf. Turn three asks about the location of the polka dot scarf which has been passed to the model on the right.
